article.title: Apple Sets WWDC 2026 for June 8, Making Tim Cook's Final Keynote a Stage for iOS 27 and a Rebuilt Siri
article.summary: Apple's 37th WWDC opens June 8 with Tim Cook delivering his last keynote before a September handoff to John Ternus, centered on a rebuilt Siri and third-party AI model integration in iOS 27.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

Apple has confirmed its 37th annual Worldwide Developers Conference will run June 8–12, 2026, with the opening keynote at 10 a.m. PDT at Apple Park in Cupertino. The event carries unusual weight this year: Tim Cook will deliver his final keynote as chief executive before stepping down on September 1 and handing leadership to John Ternus. On the technical side, WWDC 2026 is expected to deliver iOS 27, macOS 27, and five other platform updates, with the centerpiece being a ground-up rebuild of Siri powered by Google's Gemini AI and a new third-party model selection framework.

## What We Know

### The conference schedule

Apple sent media invitations on May 18, confirming the keynote at 10 a.m. PDT on June 8, followed by the Platforms State of the Union at 1 p.m. PDT the same day. Sessions and developer labs run through Friday, June 12. The event streams free on Apple.com, the Apple TV app, and YouTube, and more than 1,000 developers, designers, and students will attend in person at Apple Park.

"WWDC is one of the most exciting times for us at Apple because it's a chance for our incredible global developer community to come together for an electrifying week that celebrates technology, innovation, and collaboration," said Susan Prescott, Apple's VP of Worldwide Developer Relations, in a statement published on [Apple's Newsroom](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/05/apple-kicks-off-worldwide-developers-conference-on-june-8/). The conference will feature over 100 new video sessions alongside Group Labs, Q&A sessions, and the Apple Design Awards, which this year has 36 finalists across six categories.

On the student side, 350 Swift Student Challenge winners will be honored, and 50 Distinguished Winners have been invited to a three-day experience in Cupertino that includes time with Apple's engineers — and with Tim Cook himself.

### Tim Cook's final keynote

Apple's board voted on April 17 and publicly announced on April 21 that Cook will step down as CEO on September 1, 2026, after 15 years in the role. He will remain at Apple as Executive Chairman, while outgoing Chairman Arthur Levinson transitions to lead independent director. Ternus, 51, has served as senior VP of Hardware Engineering since 2021 and was widely recognized as Cook's heir apparent before the announcement was made official.

Cook's tenure saw Apple's market cap grow from roughly $350 billion when he took over in August 2011 to approximately $4 trillion today, and the company's quarterly services revenue expanded from roughly $2.8 billion in 2011 to $30 billion in the most recent quarter, according to [Fortune](https://fortune.com/2026/04/21/apple-ceo-tim-cook-steps-down-john-ternus/). WWDC 2026 will be the last major platform showcase he leads as the company's chief executive.

### iOS 27 and the Siri overhaul

The technical centerpiece of the keynote is expected to be a rebuilt Siri. According to [9to5Mac reporting on Bloomberg's Mark Gurman](https://9to5mac.com/2026/05/12/ios-27s-completely-rebuilt-siri-will-include-a-new-system-wide-search-gesture-report/), iOS 27 will introduce a new system-wide gesture — swiping down from the top center of the screen anywhere — that pulls up a "Search or Ask" bar in the Dynamic Island. Users will be able to toggle between Siri and third-party offerings like ChatGPT or Gemini as their search engine directly from that bar, with a microphone icon enabling voice mode.

Alongside the gesture, Apple is building a standalone Siri app with a conversational interface featuring uploads, history, and pins. Users can swipe a transparent results card down to enter a chatbot conversation mode, and the interface can display inline mini app cards showing weather, appointments, and notes. A "+" button supports uploading images and documents.

[MacRumors](https://www.macrumors.com/roundup/wwdc/) describes the expected result as a "full chatbot version of Siri that works similarly to Claude or ChatGPT," finally delivering on Apple Intelligence capabilities that were originally promised for iOS 18.

### Third-party AI model selection

A separate feature called Extensions, also [reported by 9to5Mac via Bloomberg](https://9to5mac.com/2026/05/05/ios-27-will-let-you-choose-between-gemini-claude-and-more-for-ai-features-report/), will allow users to route Apple Intelligence requests through installed third-party AI apps. Apple describes Extensions as allowing access to "generative AI capabilities from installed apps on demand, through Apple Intelligence features." Supported providers at launch are expected to include Google (Gemini) and Anthropic (Claude), with other App Store apps able to add support. The feature extends across Siri, Writing Tools, Image Playground, and broader Apple Intelligence functions. Users will also be able to assign distinct voices to each AI model, letting them audibly distinguish between Apple's native system and a third-party provider.

### Other platform updates

Apple will debut iOS 27, iPadOS 27, macOS 27, tvOS 27, watchOS 27, and visionOS 27 at the keynote. [9to5Mac notes](https://9to5mac.com/2026/03/23/apple-announces-wwdc-2026-for-june-8-ios-27-set-to-be-revealed/) the iOS 27 release is also expected to include software support for Apple's first foldable iPhone, with side-by-side apps and new sidebars. Other anticipated additions include a Visual Intelligence section in the Camera app, digital pass scanning in Wallet, and further performance optimizations. Developer betas will be available immediately after the keynote, public betas in July, and the final release in September.

## What We Don't Know

Apple has not released an official feature list for iOS 27, macOS 27, or any other platform. All pre-WWDC details above come from analyst and press reporting, primarily based on sources cited by Bloomberg's Mark Gurman. The exact scope of hardware announcements — if any — remains unconfirmed; MacRumors notes that "Apple sometimes unveils new hardware" at WWDC, with Mac Studio, Mac mini, and iMac refreshes among those rumored. Whether John Ternus will appear on stage alongside Cook for a formal handoff moment has not been indicated by Apple.

## Analysis

WWDC 2026 arrives at a convergence of institutional and technical transitions. Cook's keynotes have followed a methodical rhythm for 15 years; this one carries a ceremonial dimension that none of his developer conferences have before. At the same time, Apple faces a genuine technical reckoning: the Apple Intelligence features it announced two years ago have been slow to ship, and rivals including Google and Microsoft have moved faster on integrated AI assistants. The rebuilt Siri, if it delivers on the Gurman-sourced descriptions, would close that gap meaningfully. The Extensions framework — letting users swap in Gemini or Claude to power Apple Intelligence — is a structural shift rather than a feature addition, signaling that Apple has concluded its best path forward involves making its platform a neutral conduit for AI rather than a walled garden for its own models. How much of that story Cook chooses to frame as legacy, and how much Ternus's presence shapes the tone, will be one of the more closely watched dynamics of the June 8 keynote.
